name: TAO 4h Asymmetric Reset Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: continuation is entered after a cleaner reset-and-reclaim sequence, while trigger, risk, and exit roles remain deliberately separated so winners can pay for losers
thesis: The cycle 84 packet still demands one continuation with asymmetric exit logic and clean role separation, but the last evidence says TAO continuation families fail when the trigger is late or decorative. This version keeps TAO as the only structurally valid continuation lane, but repairs the trigger by requiring reset-and-reclaim rather than breakout theater. The management is still asymmetric, but the mechanism has to deserve it.
invalidation_condition: If TAO 4h still produces negative expectancy after a cleaner reset-and-reclaim trigger, the continuation family should be abandoned again.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price pulls back, reclaims fast structure and trend context, then continues, while exit logic preserves asymmetry by keeping trigger, risk, and exit jobs separate.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Earlier TAO continuation families failed because the trigger quality was poor, not because asymmetric exits are always invalid. A cleaner reset-and-reclaim entry gives the management concept a fairer test on the only lane where continuation has structural support.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the best fit because it remains the only lane in the basket with enough trend structure for continuation asymmetry to make sense.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The reset-and-reclaim may still happen too late in the recovery, turning continuation entries into local exhaustion buys.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If density is acceptable but winners still decay, shorten the time-based de-risk step before touching the reclaim trigger.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A modest number of continuation attempts, many small wins/losses, and a smaller number of larger winners carrying expectancy.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h clears density and the reset-and-reclaim version materially improves expectancy over the previously failed continuation family.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-12 trades, or another clearly negative expectancy profile despite adequate count.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because reset-and-reclaim should be denser than delayed boundary-touch continuation

test_lanes.why_included: Best-fit lane for role-separated asymmetric continuation after a cleaner reset-and-reclaim trigger.
test_lanes.continue_if: TAO 4h shows enough continuation attempts with materially improved asymmetry.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h still produces negative expectancy despite adequate count.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Moderate participation with a few larger winners carrying expectancy.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the same reset-and-reclaim continuation survives on the primary transition lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: BTC 4h preserves enough continuation structure with the same role separation.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: BTC 4h confirms the concept still needs stronger trend contamination.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: More frequent but smaller asymmetry opportunities than TAO.

test_lanes.why_included: Useful control to test whether pure chop still suppresses the same continuation logic.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH 4h prints some orderly continuation attempts.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h confirms the concept remains anti-chop there.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Sparse and smaller continuation carry than TAO.

long: Next bar low stays >= EMA_20 - 0.07 * ATR_14
short: Next bar high stays <= EMA_20 + 0.07 * ATR_14

long: Trail remaining 60% by 0.78 * ATR_14 while close remains >= EMA_55
Time-based de-risk: cut 20% after 6 bars if no 1.0 ATR follow-through
short: Trail remaining 60% by 0.78 * ATR_14 while close remains <= EMA_55
Time-based de-risk: cut 20% after 6 bars if no 1.0 ATR follow-through
